title: Playbook - Qualys VMDR Host Detections Export
applies_to: Export host vulnerability detections from Qualys VMDR
prerequisites: Correct Qualys platform base URL for the subscription
Working authentication using the subscription's enabled auth mode
Decision on XML parsing strategy before ingestion
required_permissions: Qualys account access sufficient to view target hosts and detections
Product-specific permissions for VM/VMDR reporting as applicable
request_sequence: Confirm the correct Qualys API server URL for the tenant
Authenticate using Basic Auth or an enabled JWT/OIDC flow for the subscription
Call the Host Detection List API for the target host scope
Parse the XML response and normalize hosts, QIDs, severity, and status fields
Layer dashboard or report workflows separately if the consumer needs presentation-friendly output

confirmed_facts.fact: Host detection retrieval is part of the classic VM API host list family.
Qualys API server URLs are subscription-specific and must match the account's platform.
Classic VM reporting and detection APIs are XML-oriented.

implementation_notes: Do not assume modern JSON conventions for classic VMDR exports.
Design an explicit XML-to-tabular normalization pass; do not mix transport parsing with business logic.
Treat dashboards as a secondary derivation from raw detection data unless a specific report API is required.
common_failures: Authentication succeeds against the wrong Qualys pod but returns misleading errors for downstream APIs
XML parsing breaks when optional nodes are missing
Large exports become slow if host scope is not constrained
